title: Observability, SLOs, Drift, Incidents, and Recovery · Behavior Case: Rag Incident Causal Recovery
summary: A RAG endpoint records total latency and full prompts, cannot distinguish retrieval from generation failure, and notices stale results only after users complain. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the incident causal recovery increment in redacted stage trace and reliability dashboard. Learners progress through…
objectives: Preserve first evidence, classify the failed stage, contain exposure, restore known versions, replay safe work, verify recovery, and update tests.
Monitor corpus, query, language, embedding, score, filter, feedback, answerability, citation, latency, and cost drift with labeled confirmation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-incident-causal-recovery — Preserve first evidence, classify the failed stage, contain exposure, restore known versions, replay safe work, verify recovery, and update tests.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-observability-reliability-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-observability-reliability-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_observability_reliability_5(trace_stages: int = 6, expected_stages: int = 6, retrieval_p95_ms: float = 42.0, error_rate: float = 0.01, content_logged: bool = False, rollback_ready: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if trace_stages != expected_stages or expected_stages < 5:
        return False, trace_stages, "trace-completeness"
    if retrieval_p95_ms <= 0.0 or error_rate < 0.0 or error_rate > 1.0:
        return False, error_rate, "service-signal"
    if content_logged:
        return False, error_rate, "sensitive-telemetry"
    if not rollback_ready:
        return False, error_rate, "recovery-readiness"
    assert trace_stages == expected_stages
    return True, round(retrieval_p95_ms, 2), "redacted-stage-trace"

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Incident Causal Recovery model · Theory Rag Incident Causal Recovery
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Incident Causal Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 5117 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to trace every RAG stage with bounded redacted signals, SLOs, causal faults, and rollback readiness; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve first evidence, classify the failed stage, contain exposure, restore known versions, replay safe work, verify recovery, and update tests.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Incident Causal Recovery model · Theory Rag Incident Causal Recovery.
steps.why: Retrieving Rag Incident Causal Recovery through the concrete redacted stage trace and reliability dashboard case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Incident Causal Recovery model · Theory Rag Incident Causal Recovery, keep Rag Incident Causal Recovery connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve first evidence, classify the failed stage, contain exposure, restore known versions, replay safe work, verify recovery, and update tests.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve first evidence, classify the failed stage, contain exposure, restore known versions, replay safe work, verify recovery, and update tests.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Incident Causal Recovery model · Theory Rag Incident Causal Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Switching to a larger model is the safest generic RAG incident response.
